title: Acrocentron salonitanum
taxonomy: Acrocentron salonitanum is classified as a species within the genus Acrocentron of the family Apocynaceae. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleander family. This family consists of over 5,000 species, which are primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Acrocentron salonitanum's classification is further detailed as belonging to the order Gentianales, which is a large and widespread order of flowering plants. The classification of Acrocentron salonitanum is a crucial aspect of understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological role.
uses: Acrocentron salonitanum is a plant that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although limited research is available. benefits: Acrocentron salonitanum, a plant species native to the Himalayan region, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions.
